text: Gold PPO: $500/month premium, $2000 annual deductible, 10% coinsurance, network tier: Gold, coverage type: PPO.
Silver HMO: $300/month premium, $1500 annual deductible, 20% coinsurance, network tier: Silver, coverage type: HMO.
Bronze HMO: $150/month premium, $1000 annual deductible, 30% coinsurance, network tier: Bronze, coverage type: HMO.

Summary of Benefits and Coverage Sample Health Plan: Gold PPO 500 (Synthetic Example) This is a synthetic Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) document created for training and demonstration purposes only. It does not represent a real insurance plan or any real member's coverage. Important Questions Question Answer What is the overall deductible? $2,000 individual / $4,000 family. Applies to most services. Are there other deductibles for specific services? No other specific deductibles. Is

services? No other specific deductibles. Is there an out-of-pocket limit on my expenses? Yes. $6,000 individual / $12,000 family. What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit? Premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn't cover. Do I need a referral to see a specialist? No, you do not need a referral to see a specialist. Common Medical Events Service What You Will Pay (In-Network) Primary care visit $25 copay per visit Specialist visit $50 copay per visit Preventive

Specialist visit $50 copay per visit Preventive care/screening/immunization No charge Diagnostic test (x-ray, blood work) 10% coinsurance after deductible Imaging (CT/PET scans, MRIs) 10% coinsurance after deductible Generic drugs $10 copay Outpatient surgery 10% coinsurance after deductible Emergency room care $250 copay, then 10% coinsurance
Excluded Services Cosmetic surgery, dental care (adult), long-term care, and weight loss programs are generally not covered under this plan.

Claims Process Guide This document is a synthetic example describing how to file a health insurance claim. It is created for training and demonstration purposes only and does not represent any real member's information. Step 1: Confirm Your Coverage Before filing a claim, confirm that the service you received is covered under your plan. Review your Summary of Benefits and Coverage document or contact member services if you are unsure. Step 2: Gather Required Documents You will need the following

Required Documents You will need the following documents to file a claim: Itemized bill from the provider Proof of payment, if applicable Your member ID number A completed claim form Step 3: Submit Your Claim Claims can be submitted online through the member portal, by mail, or by fax. Online submission is the fastest method and typically results in a decision within 5-10 business days. Step 4: Track Claim Status Once submitted, a claim will show one of the following statuses: Pending,

will show one of the following statuses: Pending, Approved, or Denied. Pending claims are still under review. Approved claims will be paid according to your plan's coverage terms. Denied claims will include a reason and instructions for filing an appeal. Step 5: Appeals Process If a claim is denied and you believe it should be covered, you may file an appeal within 180 days of the denial notice. Appeals should include any additional documentation supporting medical necessity.
